pattern: A stack of unmatched-bracket indices lets each closing bracket measure the span back to the last position that could not be matched, giving the longest balanced run.
hints: A substring is valid only if every prefix has at least as many opens as closes and the totals match; brute force checks every substring but is slow.
Track positions, not just counts: when a close has no partner, it becomes a new barrier that future valid spans cannot cross.
Push -1 then every '(' index onto a stack; on ')', pop, and if the stack is now empty push this index as a new base, else the current length is i minus the new stack top.

edge_inputs.description: trailing valid pair after an unmatched open
leading unmatched close then a valid run
empty string has no valid run
nested then separate valid spans joined
valid run broken by a stray close in the middle

stress.description: random parenthesis strings biased so long valid runs can appear
stress.note: slight bias toward opens lengthens valid runs without making them trivial
